novel_features.name: UCP merchant manifest validator
novel_features.description: Fetch `/.well-known/ucp` for any domain and return a graded report covering schema validity, advertised transports, and capability coverage — works against any merchant publishing a UCP manifest, including MCP-only Shopify-hosted stores.
novel_features.rationale: Existing UCP tooling requires a configured merchant deployment; this CLI grades any public domain in one call so an agent can decide whether to engage before committing to a transaction flow.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent screen a domain for UCP viability without standing up an SDK or hand-rolling manifest parsing.

novel_features.name: Bundled pure-Go mock UCP merchant
novel_features.description: Spawn a UCP-compliant reference merchant locally (pure-Go, no external runtime) so `ucp check`, `search`, `cart`, and `checkout prep` flows work end-to-end without a third-party UCP merchant or extra language toolchains.
novel_features.rationale: No other CLI ships a runnable merchant in-binary; existing UCP work requires deploying a server. The pure-Go mock means a single `go install` gets you a working end-to-end loop.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent verify its UCP integration without coordinating with a live merchant or waiting for Google AI Mode approval.

narrative.headline: A Go CLI for Google's Universal Commerce Protocol — validate UCP merchant manifests, run REST searches, build local carts, and prep AP2-ready checkout drafts against any UCP merchant.
narrative.value_prop: UCP-pp-cli is the terminal-grade tool the official Python and JS SDKs don't ship. It grades any UCP merchant manifest in one call, transacts against REST-transport merchants, and bundles a pure-Go reference merchant so an agent can prove its UCP wiring works end-to-end without deploying a server.
narrative.auth_narrative: UCP has no global API key. Each merchant declares its own auth shape; the CLI identifies itself via the `UCP-Agent: profile="<url>"` header and signs requests with a per-agent ECDSA-P256 key. Run `ucp profile init` once to generate your profile. For account-linked flows, `ucp auth link <merchant>` walks an OAuth 2.0 flow per merchant (v1: print-mode — agent pastes the redirect URL back).

quickstart.comment: Run in another terminal — pure-Go reference merchant, no external deps.
Add a line item; creates a new cart and prints its ID.

quickstart.command: ucp-pp-cli checkout prep --cart $(ucp-pp-cli cart list --json | jq -r '.[0].id')
quickstart.comment: Produce a checkout draft (JSON) ready for an AP2 CLI to authorize.

troubleshoots.fix: The domain hosts a UCP docs site, not a UCP merchant. Confirm the merchant publishes `/.well-known/ucp`; many real-world UCP retailers (Etsy, Wayfair) are gated to approved Google/Microsoft agents and won't respond to unapproved profiles.
The merchant only advertises MCP (e.g., coffeecircle.com). Re-run with `--transport mcp` or omit; the CLI picks the negotiated intersection automatically.
Run `ucp auth link <merchant>` first, or skip account-linking with `--guest` if the merchant allows guest checkout (declared in its manifest).

troubleshoots.symptom: mock serve fails with 'address already in use'
troubleshoots.fix: Port 8080 is taken. Pass `--port 9090` (or any free port) and update downstream commands to point at the new address.

narrative.when_to_use: Use ucp-pp-cli when an agent needs to interact with UCP merchants directly: probe a manifest, build a multi-merchant cart, run a search across known UCP-supporting stores, or prep a checkout draft for an AP2 mandate. It is the natural local pair to an AP2 CLI for the search-and-buy half of an agentic shopping flow. Skip it when you only need ChatGPT-side checkout (use an ACP-flavored tool) or when you only need merchant-side validation (use awesomeucp/ucp-doctor).

recipes.title: Boot a UCP test environment from scratch
recipes.explanation: Start the bundled pure-Go reference merchant; then run `check 127.0.0.1:8080` from another shell.

recipes.title: End-to-end search-to-checkout against the mock merchant
recipes.explanation: Fan out a query, normalize results, and pipe the first product into `cart add` then `checkout prep`.

recipes.title: Validate a real public UCP merchant manifest
recipes.explanation: Probe a Shopify-hosted UCP merchant. Returns a graded report even though the merchant advertises MCP-only transport (REST-only commands won't transact against it in v0.1).
